claim.statement: The BDBV Stage Two R prior gamma(alpha=4.0, beta=3.0), mean about 1.33, is grounded in BDBV-specific R0 estimates.

sources.citation: Wamala JF, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010; 16(7):1087-1092. DOI 10.3201/eid1607.091525.
sources.finding: Supports BDBV case count, CFR, incubation, and inter-case interval. Does not report R0.

sources.citation: Van Kerkhove MD, et al. Scientific Data 2015;2:150019. DOI 10.1038/sdata.2015.19.
sources.finding: Review states: 'R0 has not been estimated for Ebola Bundibugyo.'

sources.citation: Lekone PE, Finkenstadt BF. Biometrics 2006;62(4):1170-1177. DOI 10.1111/j.1541-0420.2006.00609.x.
sources.finding: Stochastic SEIR framework reference only. It predates BDBV discovery and does not ground a 2007 Bundibugyo R0 estimate.

steps.finding: Wamala 2010 contains the outbreak shape used by the prior: 116 cases, CFR 34%, median incubation 7 days, and 3 successive cycles. No R0 parameter is reported.
The 2015 Scientific Data parameter review explicitly says BDBV R0 had not been estimated. This contradicts treating the gamma prior as BDBV-specific literature-grounded.
The parameterization is internally coherent: mean = 4.0 / 3.0 = 1.33 and standard deviation = sqrt(4.0) / 3.0 = 0.67.
No direct BDBV R0 source was found. Keep the value only as a clearly labeled interim modeling prior, or rederive from a verified source before claiming primary-source grounding.

chains.next_action: Do not cite gamma(4.0, 3.0) as BDBV-specific R0 evidence until a direct source or explicit derivation is added.

claim.statement: Module C reporting-completeness interval is derived from a single peer-reviewed onset-to-notification gamma cited from Camacho 2015, applied cross-species from EBOV-Zaire to BDBV as best available proxy.
claim.value: gamma(0.81, 0.18) shape-rate, mean 4.5 d, s.d. 5 d

sources.citation: Camacho A, et al. PLOS Currents Outbreaks 2015. DOI 10.1371/currents.outbreaks.406ae55e83ec0b5193e30856b9235ed2.
sources.finding: Appendix 1 reports the empirical EBOV-Zaire onset-to-notification distribution as over-dispersed with mean 4.5 d, s.d. 5 d. Methods state Camacho's own dynamic model uses an over-dispersed delay with average 4.3 d. The 4.5 d / 5 d s.d. parameters ground the corrected TOTAL_DELAY_GAMMA via method-of-moments.

sources.citation: Cori A, et al. American Journal of Epidemiology 2013;178(9):1505-1512. DOI 10.1093/aje/kwt133.
sources.finding: EpiEstim methodology paper. Does not source any presentation-to-report delay distribution. Removed from PRIOR_CITATIONS as part of the correction.

sources.citation: WHO Ebola Response Team. New England Journal of Medicine 2014;371:1481-1495. DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa1411100.
sources.finding: Reports incubation 11.4 d and serial interval 15.3 d in the accessible abstract. Full text was 403 paywalled. Does not source a report-to-confirmation 3-day delay. Removed from PRIOR_CITATIONS as part of the correction.

steps.finding: Initial audit found the 34-41 percent output internally derived from three gammas summing by method-of-moments to gamma(7.5, 1.0), but the three stage-specific source attributions did not support the stated gamma parameters.

Pre-correction: gamma_cdf(7, 7.5, 1.0) is approximately 0.4745; Beta-Binomial post mean is 53/657 which is approximately 0.081; mixed 3 to 1 yields 0.376, matching the displayed [0.340, 0.412] interval. Post-correction: gamma_cdf(7, 0.81, 0.18) is approximately 0.786; mixed 3 to 1 with 0.081 yields 0.610, matching the new [0.591, 0.626] interval.

Collapsed the three-stage chain to a single TOTAL_DELAY_GAMMA = (0.81, 0.18), method-of-moments matched to Camacho 2015 Appendix 1 (mean 4.5 d, s.d. 5 d). Removed ONSET_TO_PRESENTATION_GAMMA, PRESENTATION_TO_REPORT_GAMMA, REPORT_TO_CONFIRMATION_GAMMA. Trimmed PRIOR_CITATIONS to Camacho alone with an explicit EBOV-Zaire to BDBV cross-species acknowledgment. Reduced alpha_sigma from 0.5 to 0.10 and the sampled-alpha floor from 0.5 to 0.1 to keep relative shape-parameter uncertainty consistent with the new alpha. Bumped MODEL_VERSION to lovs_visibility-v0.2.0. Re-ran refresh_pipeline.py and tools/sync_to_website.py: snapshot now shows visibility.grade low, reportingCompleteness50 [0.591, 0.626], publicationLatencyDays50 [1.1, 6.6].
2026-05-21 recheck against the open PDF confirmed the exact Appendix statement: onset-to-notification mean 4.5 days and s.d. 5 days. Method-of-moments gamma(0.81, 0.18) remains source-backed.

chains.next_action: Keep Module C delay prior linked to this chain. Follow-ups: re-derive any website prose or brief copy referencing 34-41 percent or very_low; revisit Camacho 2015 ref [10] to deepen citation; evaluate Module D corridor-risk visibility-adjustment factor change from approximately 2.95x to approximately 1.69x.

claim.statement: The BDBV prior-outbreak CFR scenario band is 26/33/40 percent (central 33), per the US CDC 55/169 aggregate and the Imperial 20 May 2026 update. It supersedes the 18 May 24/30/40 set and the older unsupported 30-50 percent range.
claim.value: CFR scenarios 0.26 / 0.33 / 0.40 (central 0.33)

sources.citation: Wamala JF, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010;16(7):1087-1092. DOI 10.3201/eid1607.091525.
sources.finding: Reports the 2007-2008 Uganda BDBV outbreak CFR as 34 percent (39/116 = 33.6).

sources.citation: US CDC. History of Ebola Outbreaks.
sources.finding: Aggregates prior BDBV outbreaks as 55 deaths and 169 cases (32.5 percent central).

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the BDBV outbreak in DRC: 20 May 2026 update.
sources.finding: Corrects the 18 May 24/30/40 percent CFR set to 26/33/40, with central 32.5 percent (55/169) and the 26-40 band as the Wilson 95% CI; deaths input updated from 88 to 131.

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in DRC. 18 May 2026 (superseded).
sources.finding: The superseded 18 May report used 24, 30, and 40 percent CFR scenarios; its 30 percent central did not match the 55/169 = 32.5 percent aggregate.

steps.finding: Wamala supports the point estimate 39 / 116 = 33.6 percent, not a 30-50 percent range.
CDC aggregate 55 / 169 = 32.5 percent central (rounds to 33). Wilson 95% CI of 55/169 is [25.9 percent, 39.9 percent], which rounds to the 26 / 40 percent scenario bounds.

2026-05-21: superseded the 18 May 24/30/40 set with the Imperial 20 May update 26/33/40 (central 33). Updated lovs/lovs_death_back_projection.py (IMPERIAL_CFR_SCENARIOS, CENTRAL_CFR) and reconciled README.md, CITATIONS.md, NUMBERS_AUDIT.md, make_brief.py, and tools/sync_to_website.py. The companion-site InferredTrajectory.tsx / methodology_constants surface updates on the next tools/sync_to_website.py run.

chains.next_action: Keep the CFR band linked to this chain at 26/33/40 (central 33). Re-sync the website snapshot's methodology_constants so the live InferredTrajectory band reflects 26/40. Avoid reintroducing the 24/30/40 set or the unsupported 30-50 percent range.

claim.statement: The BDBV Stage Two detection-depth priors are source-backed where possible: serial interval is bracketed by Wamala 2010, incubation is anchored by MacNeil 2010 and Wamala 2010, Albariño 2013 supports same-species consistency only, and under-ascertainment remains an explicit heuristic range.
claim.value: serial gamma(4.0, 0.55), incubation gamma(4.0, 0.6), under_ascertainment_uniform(0.3, 0.9)

sources.citation: Wamala JF, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010;16(7):1087-1092. DOI 10.3201/eid1607.091525.
sources.finding: Reports median incubation 7 days (range 2-20) and three successive transmission cycles with 3-11 day intervals.

sources.citation: MacNeil A, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010;16(12):1969-1972. DOI 10.3201/eid1612.100627.
sources.finding: Reports mean incubation 6.3 days among laboratory-confirmed cases (n=24), with survivors 5.7 days and fatal cases 7.4 days.

sources.citation: Albariño CG, et al. Virology 2013;442(2):97-100. DOI 10.1016/j.virol.2013.05.001.
sources.finding: Confirms the 2012 DRC outbreak as Bundibugyo virus emergence in DRC by genomic analysis. It does not report serial interval, incubation, or ascertainment parameters.

steps.finding: Wamala supports a BDBV inter-case interval bracket of 3-11 days and median incubation 7 days.
MacNeil supports the incubation anchor: mean 6.3 days in 24 laboratory-confirmed patients.
Albariño is valid only as a same-species/genomic consistency check for the 2012 DRC BDBV cluster. It must not be used as evidence for numeric transmission-delay parameters.

serial gamma(4.0, 0.55) has mean 7.27 d and s.d. 3.64 d, bracketing Wamala's 3-11 d interval. incubation gamma(4.0, 0.6) has mean 6.67 d and s.d. 3.33 d, consistent with MacNeil mean 6.3 d and Wamala range 2-20 d.
under_ascertainment_uniform(0.3, 0.9) is retained as a heuristic early-outbreak surveillance range, not as a BDBV-specific primary-source parameter.

chains.next_action: Keep serial and incubation priors source-linked; do not describe Albariño or the under-ascertainment uniform as direct numeric grounding.

claim.statement: The Module D corridor gravity exponents, normalizers, and clamp are literature-grounded parameter values.
claim.value: log-population product / 100, road product / 25, healthcare log distance / 5, conflict multiplier, clamp [0.1, 10.0]

sources.citation: Backer JA, Wallinga J. PLOS Computational Biology 2016;12(12):e1005210. DOI 10.1371/journal.pcbi.1005210.
sources.finding: Supports the family of gravity-type spatial-dispersion models for WA 2014 Ebola and reports district-level spatiotemporal data, but does not support LOVS's exact covariate exponents or clamps.

sources.citation: Glynn JR, et al. Journal of Infectious Diseases 2018;217(2):232-237. DOI 10.1093/infdis/jix579.
sources.finding: Supports household secondary attack rate magnitude (18% in the open PDF), not a cross-prefecture per-case hazard coefficient or gravity exponent set.

sources.citation: Faye O, et al. Lancet Infectious Diseases 2015;15(3):320-326. DOI 10.1016/S1473-3099(14)71075-8.
sources.finding: Supports setting-specific reproduction estimates in Conakry chains, not LOVS corridor exponents or the 100x household-to-cross-prefecture scaling.

sources.citation: Camacho A, et al. PLOS Currents Outbreaks 2015. DOI 10.1371/currents.outbreaks.406ae55e83ec0b5193e30856b9235ed2.
sources.finding: Supports a stochastic SEIR/reporting-delay framework for Sierra Leone, not LOVS corridor gravity exponents.

steps.finding: Backer and Wallinga support the general gravity-type spatial-dispersion family using distance and population size, so the broad modeling family is defensible.
Glynn supports household SAR magnitude, but LOVS's per-case cross-prefecture coefficient 0.003 and the approximately 100x down-scaling are heuristic.
The edge-weight formula is internally deterministic and bounded, but the constants /100, /25, /5, target conflict multiplier, and [0.1, 10.0] clamp are not fitted or quoted from any cited source.
No source-backed exponent fit exists in the repo. Treat Module D corridor exponents as transparent engineering heuristics until fitted against a validated mobility/spatial-spread dataset.

chains.next_action: Do not present the current corridor exponents as literature-grounded. Fit or calibrate them before claiming source-backed corridor discrimination.

claim.statement: The WA 2014 retrospective skill scores are computed from a Backer-Wallinga 62-prefecture, 74-week substrate; the reported W3-W11 evaluation window is a local deterministic protocol, not a source-imposed capture range.
claim.value: W3, W5, W7, W9, W11 as-of weeks; 4-week horizon; no-context Brier 0.0586, WIS 0.1002, ECE 0.0391

sources.citation: Backer JA, Wallinga J. PLOS Computational Biology 2016;12(12):e1005210. DOI 10.1371/journal.pcbi.1005210.
sources.finding: Supports the WA 2014 district-level Ebola spatiotemporal substrate and says relevant data are in the supporting information.

sources.citation: data/west-africa-prefecture-weekly.json metadata, derived from Backer-Wallinga S1 Data.
sources.finding: Local substrate contains 62 prefectures, 74 weekly counts per prefecture, source SHA-256 cc2ae026ec7d2ce73977a05d727b14db93d0dc110b4455c2d7b88a7879ece663, and declaration-week alignment rationale.

sources.citation: lovs/lovs_validation.py deterministic Mode A backtest.
sources.finding: Defines as_of_weeks=(3,5,7,9,11), horizon_weeks=4, and computes deterministic Brier/WIS/ECE scores.

steps.finding: Backer-Wallinga supports use of weekly district-level WA 2014 incidence through June 2015; the local archive concretizes this as 62 prefectures by 74 weeks.
Local JSON shape check: 62 prefectures, all with 74 weekly counts; 56 prefectures ever active.
The pending-queue phrase '1 to 11 week capture range' was imprecise. The code and reproduced output use W3, W5, W7, W9, W11 as-of dates, not W1-W11.

chains.next_action: Keep the W3-W11 window described as a local validation protocol; cite Backer-Wallinga only for the substrate, not for the window choice.

claim.artifact: not redistributed: restricted local review notes for Imperial Table 3
claim.locator: Nord Kivu and Ituri+Nord Kivu Table 3 row-sum check
claim.statement: Imperial Table 3's Nord Kivu summary total can be adjudicated against the underlying WHO epiweek 10/11/15/18 sitreps.
claim.value: Imperial's published Nord Kivu PoE summary total does not reconcile with the sum of its own Table 3 per-PoE rows (off by 18 daily travellers); absolute counts are restricted and not redistributed here

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in DRC. 18 May 2026.
sources.finding: Local PDF Table 3 gives per-PoE rows and summary totals. It cites WHO-shared PoE data, not the underlying raw sitreps.

sources.citation: WHO AFRO Weekly External Situation Report 01, data as of 18 May 2026.
sources.finding: Situation report landing page and downloadable PDF are accessible, but the PDF does not contain the epiweek 10/11/15/18 point-of-entry passenger table needed to adjudicate Imperial Table 3.

sources.citation: Restricted local review notes for Imperial Table 3; not redistributed in the public repository.
sources.finding: The public repository no longer redistributes the extracted PoE table. Only the discrepancy summary and source attribution remain in the evidence chain.

steps.finding: The Ituri per-PoE rows reconcile to Imperial's declared Ituri total. The Nord Kivu per-PoE rows sum to 18 daily travellers above Imperial's declared Nord Kivu total, and the combined total inherits the same 18-traveller discrepancy. The individual per-PoE passenger counts are restricted Imperial Table 3 data and are not redistributed here.
Imperial Table 3 states the PoE data came from four one-week sitreps covering epiweeks 10, 11, 15, and 18, shared by WHO on 16 May 2026.

The public WHO AFRO sitrep 01 PDF was retrievable on 2026-05-21 (SHA-256 b43fc698bf2407323c6d6af25a3679eccdd2d68b3effc61a7563e6ef977366ba), but text extraction found only general PoE preparedness language, not the passenger-count table.
The underlying WHO epiweek passenger-count sitreps are still not in the repo or public WHO sitrep 01 PDF, so the discrepancy cannot be adjudicated beyond row-sum correction.

chains.next_action: Do not redistribute the extracted Table 3 PoE dataset. Reopen only if WHO publishes the underlying epiweek 10/11/15/18 PoE passenger-count sitreps, Imperial grants permission, or Imperial issues an erratum.

claim.locator: entries[].license for WHO-published sources (who-don602, who-pheic, afro-sitrep)
claim.statement: WHO Disease Outbreak News and situation-report content archived in this repo is licensed CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO.
claim.value: CC-BY-NC-SA-3.0-IGO

sources.citation: World Health Organization. Copyright and permissions policy. who.int/about/policies/publishing/copyright.
sources.finding: States that publications published by WHO are licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO. The more permissive CC BY 3.0 IGO and CC BY-NC 3.0 IGO apply only to WHO-staff or WHO-funded articles in non-WHO publications, not to WHO's own Disease Outbreak News.

sources.citation: WHO Disease Outbreak News item 2026-DON602, archived 2026-05-20 (SHA-256 8b7fb1e1c8403b7a6015c804a3cd818c04b649ca23d791fe957e59119818218f).
sources.finding: The DON page footer shows a bare copyright line ('(c) 2026 WHO') and a Terms-of-use link rather than an inline CC badge; the applicable license is WHO's standard publications license CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO per the copyright policy.

steps.finding: WHO copyright policy: publications published by WHO are CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO (attribution, non-commercial, share-alike).

The manifest, the WHO_DON_602_TARGET ingest target in lovs/lovs_live_ingest.py, and the test fixtures previously recorded CC-BY-3.0-IGO for WHO sources. That is WHO's permissive staff-article license, not the publications license, and it understated WHO's reserved NonCommercial and ShareAlike rights.

Changed every WHO-publisher license field from CC-BY-3.0-IGO to CC-BY-NC-SA-3.0-IGO: data/bundibugyo-2026/manifest.json (3 entries), lovs/lovs_live_ingest.py WHO_DON_602_TARGET, tests/data/lovs/fixture/manifest.json (5 entries), tests/test_lovs_live_ingest.py. Prose in README.md, CITATIONS.md, make_brief.py, LICENSES.md, NOTICE, and the website footer already used CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO and is now consistent across machine and human fields.

chains.next_action: Reconciled. Reopen only if WHO publishes a page-level CC notice for Disease Outbreak News that differs from the standard CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO publications license.

claim.statement: The archived Imperial College MRC GIDA 18 May 2026 report is recorded under a restricted-use CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 assumption pending the primary license footer.
claim.value: CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0

sources.citation: Archived Imperial MRC GIDA report landing page; local archive content hash SHA-256 647610bf3450407ec3313e2a17642b0ee9cbec7f14c72665318993f06acaf8b4.
sources.finding: The archived bytes are the report landing-page HTML. Footer shows only a bare copyright ('(c) Imperial College London'); there is no inline Creative Commons notice. The report PDF that would carry the license footer is not byte-archived in this repo.

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, main site, checked 2026-05-20.
sources.finding: Site footer shows only '(c) 2026 Imperial College London' with no site-level Creative Commons license; per-report license terms live in each report PDF, which is not held here.

steps.finding: Grep of the archived landing-page bytes returns a generic copyright mention and no Creative Commons / by-nc-nd string.

The live MRC GIDA site likewise exposes no site-level CC license. Repo prose and the optional PoE helper consistently record CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 from the report's stated terms at access time; extracted Table 3 data is no longer redistributed in the public tree.

Replaced the non-standard manifest label 'imperial-college-research-output' with 'CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0' so the machine field matches the repo-wide restricted-use assumption. Added a manifest license_note documenting the confirmation gap. The repository should treat Imperial-derived table data and archived publisher bytes as restricted third-party material until the primary license footer or permission is archived.

The authoritative license footer is in the report PDF, which is not byte-archived; the landing page and live site do not restate the CC variant. The exact license string therefore cannot be re-confirmed from repo-held bytes.

chains.next_action: Byte-archive the Imperial report PDF (or its license footer) to confirm CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 directly. Until then the value is the conservative assumption, not a byte-verified quote.

claim.statement: The May 2026 BDBV staged count observations preserve separate confirmed, probable, suspected, suspected-death, and approximate-media denominators instead of treating CDC, WHO speech, ECDC, and aggregator values as interchangeable.
claim.value: CDC 2026-05-21 exact tuple; ECDC 2026-05-21 WHO-derived regional cross-check; CDC 2026-05-20/data-as-of 2026-05-19 exact tuple; WHO DG 2026-05-20 confirmed endpoint and approximate suspected burden; aggregator values cross-check only.

sources.citation: US CDC. Ebola Disease: Current Situation. 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Reports, as of 21 May, 575 suspected cases, 51 confirmed cases, and 148 suspected deaths; Uganda remains two confirmed imported cases including one death with no further spread reported.

sources.citation: US CDC. Ebola Disease: Current Situation. 20 May 2026.
sources.finding: Reports, as of 19 May, 536 suspected cases, 105 probable cases, 34 confirmed cases, and 134 suspected deaths; also reports 11 DRC health zones in Ituri and Nord-Kivu as of 20 May.

sources.citation: WHO Director-General's opening remarks at the media briefing on Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda, 20 May 2026.
sources.finding: Reports 51 confirmed DRC cases, two confirmed Uganda cases, almost 600 suspected cases, and 139 suspected deaths.

sources.citation: WHO Disease Outbreak News DON602, Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC and Uganda, 16 May 2026.
sources.finding: Provides the May 15 baseline: 246 suspected cases and 80 deaths in DRC, Bundibugyo confirmation, and initial affected health zones.

sources.citation: ECDC. Ebola virus disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, 19 May 2026.
sources.finding: Reports 30 laboratory-confirmed cases, over 500 suspected cases, and 130 deaths; useful as a regional cross-check rather than a replacement for CDC/WHO denominators.

sources.citation: ECDC. Ebola virus disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, updated 21 May 2026 18:00.
sources.finding: Reports WHO-derived values as of 20 May: approximately 600 suspected cases, 139 deaths among suspected cases, 51 confirmed DRC cases, and two imported Uganda cases. Use as regional cross-check, not as a new primary line list.

steps.finding: CDC's 21 May Current Situation page is the current official structured tuple. It omits a probable-case field, so staged observations record suspected, confirmed, and suspected-deaths denominators without fabricating a probable value.
CDC is the cleanest structured official tuple for data as of 19 May and must be staged as four distinct denominators: suspected, probable, confirmed, and suspected deaths.
WHO's 20 May remarks provide a later confirmed endpoint and approximate suspected burden; the 'almost 600' phrasing must not be coerced into an exact numeric input.
The staged May 19 tuple 536/105/34/134 had been attributed to ECDC, but that exact tuple is from CDC Current Situation. The observed staging now uses source_id cdc-current-situation-2026-05-20.

The 21 May ECDC update corroborates the WHO 20 May confirmed split (51 DRC + 2 Uganda) and suspected-deaths endpoint (139) while retaining approximate suspected-case semantics. It is staged as cross_check_only and does not override the CDC 21 May structured tuple or the higher unresolved public suspected-count signal.

chains.next_action: May 21 CDC source is archived and model-eligible after release. Keep denominator-specific staged observations, and hold any May 22 snapshot until an official source with published_at 2026-05-22 or later is archived.

claim.statement: The reported South Kivu/Bukavu BDBV signal is an AP/AFP-reported M23/AFC claim and is not confirmed by a public-health authority.
claim.value: watch only; not a model input

sources.citation: Associated Press. M23 reports Ebola case near Bukavu/South Kivu, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Wire report says M23 claimed a confirmed fatal case near Bukavu; AP is not the public-health authority for confirmation.

sources.citation: AFP via eNCA. Deadly DR Congo Ebola outbreak spreads to M23-held South Kivu, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Reports an M23 spokesman claim, notes Congolese authorities had not commented, and leaves sample origin around Bukavu uncertain.

sources.citation: WHO Director-General's opening remarks at the media briefing on Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda, 20 May 2026.
sources.finding: Official WHO geography through 20 May names Ituri and North Kivu in DRC, not South Kivu.

steps.finding: AP establishes that a South Kivu/Bukavu claim was publicly reported, but attributes it to M23.
AFP independently reports the same claim chain and explicitly notes lack of Congolese authority comment.
The latest official WHO geography in the archived source set is Ituri and North Kivu, so South Kivu remains unconfirmed for model purposes.

chains.next_action: Keep the South Kivu/Bukavu item in watch_signals only. Promote to staged_observations and geography inputs only after DRC MoH/INRB, WHO, Africa CDC, or CDC confirms it.

claim.statement: The joint WHO + Imperial College MRC GIDA total-case reference band on the inferred-trajectory chart is 400 to 900 cases in DRC (values over 1,000 not excluded), per the 20 May 2026 update, which supersedes the 18 May 400 to 800 band.
claim.value: 400 to 900 total cases (Imperial 20 May 2026 update; supersedes 18 May 400 to 800)

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the BDBV outbreak in DRC: 20 May 2026 update.
sources.finding: The 20 May update states approximately 400 to 900 total cases in DRC, with values over 1,000 not excluded, on a deaths input of 131 and the corrected 26/33/40 CFR set.

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in DRC. 18 May 2026 (superseded).
sources.finding: The superseded 18 May report published an approximately 400 to 800 case band on a deaths input of 88 and the 24/30/40 CFR set. The update raised deaths to 131 and lifted the upper envelope from 800 to 900.

sources.citation: Rosello A, et al. eLife 2015. DOI 10.7554/eLife.09015.
sources.finding: Isiro 2012 BDBV onset-to-death gamma (alpha 4.42, beta 0.388 per day, mean 11.37 d) is the distribution behind Imperial Method 2, which lets the deaths-back-projection component of the band be reproduced independently.

sources.citation: US CDC. History of Ebola Outbreaks.
sources.finding: The 55 deaths / 169 cases prior-BDBV aggregate (32.5 percent central; Wilson 95% CI [25.9, 39.9]) grounds the 26/33/40 CFR set used in the band's deaths-back-projection method.

sources.citation: data/bundibugyo-2026/manifest.json normalized_content for source_id imperial-mrc-gida-bdbv-2026-05-20-live.
sources.finding: Records the report's per-method case outputs: Method 1 population-movement 470 (Ituri) and 617 (Ituri plus Nord Kivu); Method 2 deaths-back-projection at 14-day doubling 559 / 678 / 860 for CFR 40 / 33 / 26. The published 400 to 900 is Imperial's round-number envelope over both methods, not a single formula output.

steps.finding: The 18 May report published 400 to 800 (deaths 88, CFR 24/30/40). The 20 May update explicitly supersedes it.
The 20 May update publishes 400 to 900 total cases in DRC (values over 1,000 not excluded), deaths input 131, CFR 26/33/40, suspected 516.

Manifest-recorded outputs span Method 1 470 to 617 and Method 2 559 to 860. The 900 upper rounds up from Method 2's CFR-26 output of about 860; the 400 floor sits below every recorded method point estimate (470 to 860), so it reflects Imperial's published lower envelope rather than a value reconstructable from repo bytes.

Independent reproduction of Method 2 at the 20 May deaths input (131) with the Rosello gamma and 14-day growth correction 1.7003: 131 * 1.7003 / {0.40, 0.33, 0.26} = 557 / 675 / 857, matching the manifest's 559 / 678 / 860 within about 3 cases. The deaths input rising 88 to 131 is what lifted the published upper from 800 to 900; the 400 floor held.

The 20 May report PDF is not byte-archived (manifest raw_archive_status private_restricted_bytes, raw_bytes_relpath null). The deaths-back-projection component reproduces, but the exact published 400 floor and 900 cap are Imperial's editorial rounding over both methods and cannot be byte-verified from repo-held bytes. Same posture as ec:lovs:archive:imperial-report-license:2026-05-20.

chains.next_action: Keep the 400 to 900 reference band linked to this chain. Byte-archive the 20 May report PDF or capture its figure text to upgrade from needs_primary_source. Do not present 400 to 900 as byte-verified, and do not conflate it with the snapshot's own deaths-back-projection band (612 to 942 at deaths 144), which is grounded by ec:lovs:method:death-back-projection:2026-05-21.

claim.statement: The deaths-back-projection that produces the snapshot's total-case band reproduces Imperial Method 2, with the onset-to-death growth correction taken from the Rosello 2015 BDBV gamma and the CFR set from the US CDC prior-outbreak aggregate. The implementation is original code.
claim.value: total_cases = deaths * (1 + r/beta)^alpha / CFR; r = ln(2)/tau; Rosello gamma alpha 4.42, beta 0.388 per day; growth correction 1.7003 at tau=14; May-20 band 612 to 942 at deaths 144, CFR 40 to 26

sources.citation: Rosello A, et al. eLife 2015. DOI 10.7554/eLife.09015, Table 5, Isiro 2012 outbreak fit.
sources.finding: Fits the 2012 DRC Isiro BDBV onset-to-death delay as gamma with mean 11.37 d, s.d. 5.41 d, shape 4.42, rate 0.388 per day. These are the alpha and beta in the growth correction.

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the BDBV outbreak in DRC: 20 May 2026 update.
sources.finding: Publicly describes the Method 2 analytical pathway C_T = D_T * (1 + r/beta)^alpha / CFR (unchanged from the 18 May report). LOVS reimplements the pathway and does not relicense report content.

sources.citation: US CDC. History of Ebola Outbreaks.
sources.finding: 55 deaths / 169 cases prior-BDBV aggregate gives the 26/33/40 CFR set (32.5 percent central; Wilson 95% CI [25.9, 39.9]). Shared with ec:lovs:website:cfr-band-correction:2026-05-20.

sources.citation: lovs/lovs_death_back_projection.py total_cases_from_deaths and sensitivity_grid.
sources.finding: Deterministic stdlib implementation. Defaults alpha and beta to the Rosello gamma; takes deaths, CFR, and doubling time as inputs.

steps.finding: Rosello Isiro 2012 fit: gamma(alpha 4.42, beta 0.388 per day), mean 11.37 d, s.d. 5.41 d.

Imperial Method 2 back-projects cumulative cases from cumulative deaths as C_T = D_T * (1 + r/beta)^alpha / CFR, with r = ln(2)/tau; the (1 + r/beta)^alpha factor de-biases naive D/CFR in a growing epidemic.
At tau = 14 d: r = ln(2)/14 = 0.04951 per day; r/beta = 0.04951 / 0.388 = 0.12760; growth correction (1 + 0.12760)^4.42 = 1.7003 (1.700 at 3 d.p.; integer outputs identical).
May-20 snapshot deaths 144: 144 * 1.7003 / {0.40, 0.33, 0.26} = 612 / 742 / 942, matching the NUMBERS_AUDIT deaths-back-projection table (612 lower, 942 upper).
The 26/33/40 CFR set is the CDC 55/169 aggregate central and Wilson bounds, the same grounding adopted in ec:lovs:website:cfr-band-correction:2026-05-20.

This band uses the snapshot's own death count (144, May 20) through a single Method 2 reproduction. It is distinct from the Imperial published 400 to 900 reference (deaths 131, two methods, ec:lovs:website:imperial-reference-range:2026-05-20). The band sits above Imperial's because it is fed the higher death count.

chains.next_action: Keep total_cases_from_deaths linked to this chain. Treat the 612 to 942 band as a reproduction on the snapshot death count, not as the Imperial reference. If the Rosello gamma or the doubling-time central changes, update this chain and NUMBERS_AUDIT together.

claim.statement: BDBV calibration commitments are block-scoped: a later snapshot can carry older blocks and append new blocks, while each block preserves its own pin date, original horizon, resolution timestamp, elapsed-days clock, and remaining-days clock.
claim.value: per block: horizon_days = date(resolves_at) - date(pinned_at); remaining_days = date(resolves_at) - date(snapshot_as_of)

sources.citation: data/calibration-ledger.json calibration-block:bdbv-uga-cod-2026:2026-05-20.
sources.finding: Pins the active calibration block on 2026-05-20 with horizon_days=30 and resolves_at=2026-06-19T23:59:59Z.

sources.citation: refresh_pipeline.py calibration_clock.
sources.finding: Computes elapsed_days and remaining_days from calendar-date differences without mutating the pinned horizon or risk ranges. Emits block-level metadata so future snapshots can append new blocks without relabeling carried-forward commitments.

steps.finding: date(2026-06-19) - date(2026-05-20) = 30 days, matching the immutable ledger horizon.
For snapshot as_of 2026-05-21T23:59:59Z, date(2026-06-19) - date(2026-05-21) = 29 days remaining and date(2026-05-21) - date(2026-05-20) = 1 day elapsed.

The original prediction horizon stays 30 days because it is part of the May 20 pre-commitment. The May 21 page-date clock is displayed separately so a carried-forward snapshot cannot imply a fresh 30-day forecast.
A future snapshot may append a new calibration block with a new pinned_at/resolves_at pair. The carried-forward May 20 block remains immutable and is scored on its original June 19 resolution date.

chains.next_action: Keep calibration_blocks as the public structure for future rolling calibration. If a later snapshot pins a new block, append it with a new block_id and show it alongside carried-forward blocks; never rewrite the May 20 block.

claim.statement: The public BDBV spatial map displays named public geographies, health-zone centroids, corridor endpoints, modeled corridors, and calibration corridors with explicit object role separation; it does not display household-level case GPS coordinates.
claim.value: spatial objects are observed-zone, case-site, watch-site, modeled-corridor, calibration-corridor, or omitted/non-geocoded

sources.citation: apps/site/app/bdbv-2026/_data/zones.json
sources.finding: Defines WGS84 coordinates, roles, geographic referents, and null-coordinate model artifacts for the public map layer.

sources.citation: WHO Disease Outbreak News item 2026-DON602, 15 May 2026.
sources.finding: Names the outbreak-affected health zones and describes the event geography used for observed-zone placement.

sources.citation: apps/site/app/bdbv-2026/_data/snapshots/*.json
sources.finding: Carries the frozen affected-zone list, corridor rows, calibration points, source IDs, source conflict notes, and snapshot dates used by the map.

steps.finding: The map view model maps affected zones to observed-zone points, imported/audit roles to case-site points, remaining geocoded corridor endpoints to watch-site points, corridor rows to modeled-corridor edges, and calibration matches to calibration-corridor edges.
Zone rows with null latitude or longitude, including model artifacts, are omitted from projection and surfaced in the omitted-object audit rail rather than assigned synthetic coordinates.
Coordinates are named public places or public administrative centroids. They are not household, burial, facility, or line-list GPS coordinates and must not be interpreted at that resolution.
Latest and dated map routes share the same spatial view model and MapLibre/Deck surface, so daily snapshots reuse the same evidence contract instead of duplicating map logic.

chains.next_action: Keep the map inspector focused on role, model use, and evidence chain IDs. If case-level or health-area line-list coordinates become available, add a new spatial object class and evidence chain before displaying them.

claim.locator: May 21 archive entries, staged observations, and watch_signals
claim.statement: The May 21 BDBV evidence sweep is separated into model-eligible official count evidence, cross-check count evidence, and context-only guidance/operational signals; non-count sources are not promoted into model inputs.
claim.value: Archived/chained: CDC Current Situation May 21; ECDC outbreak page and threat assessment May 21; CDC traveler-management guidance and traveler page May 21; PAHO/WHO epidemiological alert May 21; WHO AFRO Zambia readiness May 21; UK GOV/UKHSA support and traveler-monitoring May 21; IFRC May 21 operational signal URL-cited with bytes blocked by HTTP 403.

sources.citation: US CDC. Ebola Disease: Current Situation. 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Primary structured May 21 count tuple: 575 suspected cases, 51 confirmed cases, 148 suspected deaths; archived in manifest as cdc-current-situation-2026-05-21.

sources.citation: ECDC. Ebola virus disease outbreak in DRC and Uganda, updated 21 May 2026 18:00.
sources.finding: Regional cross-check update; WHO-derived approximate suspected and suspected-death context; archived as ecdc-bdbv-drc-uga-2026-05-21-live.

sources.citation: ECDC. Threat assessment brief: Ebola disease outbreak caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC and Uganda, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Risk/preparedness assessment; archived PDF as ecdc-threat-assessment-bdbv-2026-05-21-pdf and staged only as context/watch.

sources.citation: US CDC. Interim Guidance: Public Health Assessment and Management of Travelers Arriving from Affected Countries During the 2026 Ebola Outbreak, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Defines affected countries/areas and 21-day traveler management posture; archived PDF as cdc-traveler-management-guidance-2026-05-21-pdf; context-only.

sources.citation: US CDC. What Travelers Need to Know About Returning to the United States from DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Public traveler page on enhanced screening, IAD routing, and 21-day monitoring; archived as cdc-returning-travelers-info-2026-05-21-live; context-only.

sources.citation: PAHO/WHO. Epidemiological Alert: Ebola Disease due to Bundibugyo virus in DRC and Uganda, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Regional technical guidance for laboratory preparedness, IPC, and clinical management; PDF hash-recorded/restricted as paho-who-epialert-bdbv-2026-05-21-pdf; context-only unless counts are extracted later.

sources.citation: WHO AFRO. Strengthening Zambia’s Readiness for Health Emergencies: From Surveillance to Pandemic Preparedness, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Regional readiness/surveillance-capacity context tied to Ebola outbreaks in DRC and Uganda; archived as who-afro-zambia-readiness-2026-05-21-live; context-only.

sources.citation: UK Government. UK steps up support to stop spread of Ebola in eastern DRC, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Response-funding and UKHSA returning-worker/travel-route monitoring context; archived as uk-gov-ebola-eastern-drc-support-2026-05-21-live; not a count source.

sources.citation: IFRC. Ebola: IFRC scales up response in eastern DRC as regional risks grow, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Operational response/readiness context. Automated byte fetch returned HTTP 403, so it remains URL-cited and watch-only until bytes can be archived.

sources.citation: data/external_sources/freshness/bdbv-2026-2026-05-21.json generated by source_ingest.py --live-check.
sources.finding: Registry-backed sweep records fetched/failed status, hashes, detected dates, extracted counts, and no newer WHO DON/WHO AFRO SitRep/Africa CDC count update in this pass.

steps.finding: CDC is the only clean May 21 official structured count tuple found in the sweep; it is model-eligible after release and already archived in the outbreak manifest.

ECDC May 21 is archived and staged as cross-check count evidence: approximately 600 suspected, 139 suspected deaths, 51 DRC confirmed plus two Uganda imported cases. It does not replace CDC/WHO denominator handling.
ECDC threat-assessment bytes are archived. The risk/preparedness content is represented in watch_signals and not in model counts.
CDC traveler-management guidance and returning-traveler public page are archived and represented as context-only watch signals. They define monitoring/travel posture, not outbreak burden.

PAHO/WHO alert PDF is hash-recorded as restricted publisher material and represented as context-only guidance. It should not drive count/geography inputs unless a reviewer extracts explicit figures later.

WHO AFRO Zambia readiness, UK support/monitoring, and IFRC operational response are captured as non-model watch/context signals. IFRC bytes were blocked by HTTP 403, so the URL-cited signal remains open for future archiving.

The registry-backed freshness report did not find a newer WHO AFRO SitRep 02, WHO DON follow-up, or Africa CDC May 21 count update. Wikipedia live movement is flagged as aggregator-only until its source chain is verified.

chains.next_action: Before publishing the May 21 snapshot, review the new manifest entries, staged ECDC cross-check observations, and context-only watch signals. Promote no context signal into model inputs without explicit official count/geography content.

claim.locator: reported_counts and May 22 source archive entries
claim.statement: The May 22 public snapshot uses WHO 22 May official sources as the current headline endpoint while retaining CDC/ECDC May 21 and earlier sources as dated conflict/cross-check evidence.
claim.value: confirmed total 84 (82 DRC + 2 Uganda), suspected approximately 750, suspected deaths 177; DRC risk very high, regional risk high, global risk low; Uganda imported cases 2 with no documented onward transmission among contacts.

sources.citation: World Health Organization. WHO Director-General's opening remarks at the Member State information session on outbreaks of Ebola and hantavirus, 22 May 2026.
sources.finding: Primary May 22 count endpoint: 82 confirmed DRC cases, seven confirmed DRC deaths, almost 750 suspected cases, 177 suspected deaths, and two imported Uganda cases including one death; risk revised to very high nationally, high regionally, and low globally.

sources.citation: World Health Organization. First meeting of the IHR Emergency Committee regarding the BDBV epidemic in DRC and Uganda 2026 - Temporary recommendations, 22 May 2026.
sources.finding: Confirms risk framing for DRC and Uganda, states Uganda reported two confirmed imported BVD cases, and states no onward transmission among their contacts was documented as of 22 May.

sources.citation: US CDC. Ebola Disease: Current Situation, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Prior official structured tuple retained as dated conflict evidence: 575 suspected, 51 confirmed, 148 suspected deaths.

sources.citation: data/bundibugyo-2026/manifest.json entries who-dg-remarks-bdbv-2026-05-22 and who-ihr-ec-bdbv-temporary-recommendations-2026-05-22.
sources.finding: Both May 22 WHO source pages are byte-archived with SHA-256 hashes and normalized count/risk fields before the snapshot generator consumes them.

steps.finding: WHO 22 May is newer than CDC 21 May and contains the headline DRC/Uganda count endpoint; it supersedes CDC for current counts while preserving CDC as prior official evidence.
WHO 22 May reports 82 confirmed DRC cases and two imported Uganda cases; the country-scope aggregate consumed by the existing LOVS/website schema is 82 + 2 = 84 confirmed cases.
The IHR temporary recommendations corroborate the May 22 risk posture and Uganda status without adding zone-attributed DRC line-list counts.

The source manifest contains the May 22 WHO byte hashes and normalized fields before refresh_pipeline.py reads them, so generated repo/brief/website/spreadsheet outputs can be traced to archived source bytes.

chains.next_action: Treat the May 22 WHO DG source as the headline count endpoint only. Do not allocate the 51 confirmed cases that are not covered by the latest official health-zone table until WHO AFRO, DRC MoH, Uganda MoH, WHO, or Africa CDC publishes an updated zone-attributed line-list/table.

claim.locator: zone_attributed_counts, affected_zones, corridors drivers, snapshot_contract confirmed_case_partition
claim.statement: The May 22 corridor watchlist uses the official WHO AFRO per-health-zone source-load vector, not the May 22 headline confirmed aggregate; this is source-attribution lag, not missing cases.
claim.value: headline confirmed total 84; officially zone-attributed corridor source-load total 33 across 7 WHO AFRO source zones; 51 confirmed cases remain unallocated headline context; current 42-corridor adjusted 50% range is 0.4-8.9% lower bounds and 1.3-23.9% upper bounds.

sources.citation: World Health Organization, Regional Office for Africa. Ebola Bundibugyo virus disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, Weekly External Situation Report 01, 18 May 2026.
sources.finding: Latest official per-health-zone count table in the local archive: 33 confirmed cases across Bunia, Butembo, Goma, Katwa, Mongbwalu, Nyankunde, and Rwampara.

sources.citation: World Health Organization. WHO Director-General's opening remarks at the Member State information session on outbreaks of Ebola and hantavirus, 22 May 2026.
sources.finding: Newer headline endpoint: 82 confirmed DRC cases and two imported Uganda cases. It does not publish a per-health-zone allocation for the additional confirmed cases.

sources.citation: data/snapshot_contract.json generated from data/live-bdbv-2026-output.json by lovs/snapshot_contract.py.
sources.finding: Contract partition is 84 headline confirmed, 33 zone-attributed confirmed, and 51 unallocated confirmed; it gates public text, dataset rows, and corridor drivers.

sources.citation: lovs/lovs_next_zone.py source-load selection and corridor driver generation.
sources.finding: The model uses per-zone confirmed counts when zone_attributed_counts exists, and falls back to the headline aggregate only when no per-zone table exists.

sources.citation: Evidence chain ec:lovs:module-d:corridor-gravity-exponents:2026-05-21 and public claim audit row BDBV-CLAIM-005.
sources.finding: This chain supports the source-load selection, arithmetic partition, and generated watchlist range. It does not validate the current-outbreak corridor constants as fitted or literature-derived BDBV probability estimates.

steps.finding: WHO AFRO SitRep-01 is the load-bearing spatial attribution source: 33 confirmed cases across 7 health zones.
WHO May 22 headline confirmed total is 82 DRC + 2 Uganda = 84. The latest per-zone source-load table sums to 33, so 84 - 33 = 51 confirmed cases are unallocated for corridor-source purposes.
Using 33 zone-attributed cases avoids aggregate smearing. Scaling or copying the 84 headline aggregate across source zones would invent geography not present in official sources.

The generated contract validates that affected_zones equals zone_attributed_counts, corridor source zones equal zone_attributed_counts, all 42 corridor driver strings contain the correct officially zone-attributed confirmed source load, and stale high-60s corridor prose is rejected.

Interpret the 1.3-23.9% upper-bound range as a descriptive source-load-sensitive watchlist interval, not as a validated current-outbreak forecast, deployment recommendation, or fitted corridor-specific probability. This chain does not validate the current-outbreak corridor constants.

chains.next_action: Before any later snapshot, first refresh official per-zone source-load evidence. If no newer officially zone-attributed table exists, keep the 51 cases unallocated and do not derive corridor risks from the 84 headline aggregate. Do not present current-outbreak corridor constants as fitted or literature-grounded until BDBV-specific or outbreak-specific calibration is added.

claim.statement: Flowminder and HOT OSM are admissible open covariate/context sources for future LOVS sensitivity analyses, but they are not case-count sources and do not mutate the released May 22 snapshot.
claim.value: Flowminder: CC BY, DRC health-zone population/mobility through March 2026; HOT OSM: ODbL outbreak-response geospatial package dated 21-22 May 2026; both remain covariate/context inputs only.

sources.citation: Flowminder Foundation via HDX. Democratic Republic of Congo: Population and Mobility Estimates. HDX package bd5781f3-9c6a-427a-955b-ce2b59def8c3.
sources.finding: HDX metadata records CC BY licensing, resource modification on 21-22 May 2026, and a data interval from March 2020 through March 2026; suitable as a near-pre-outbreak mobility/population baseline, not a case-count source.

sources.citation: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team via HDX. DRC & Uganda - Ebola Outbreak - May 2026 - OpenStreetMap Data. HDX package 8533ecce-8f2c-4f40-ba6f-34ffbf9c0d66.
sources.finding: HDX metadata records ODbL licensing and outbreak-response buildings, roads, residential, and settlements resources dated 21-22 May 2026; suitable for map/access context only with ODbL attribution.

sources.citation: LOVS open-covariate source metadata file, 23 May 2026.
sources.finding: Machine-readable metadata records package IDs, resource IDs, license titles, data vintage, and handling constraints without redistributing raw Flowminder CSV or HOT OSM ZIP data.

sources.citation: LOVS recurring source registry, 23 May 2026.
sources.finding: Registry rows classify Flowminder as population/mobility covariate input and HOT OSM as geospatial context, with explicit non-count notes and non-outbreak-manifest archive targets.

steps.finding: Flowminder package licensing permits public derived use with attribution; resource vintage is March 2026, so analysis must frame it as structural mobility/population context rather than live outbreak movement.
HOT OSM package is ODbL; any map/access derivative must carry ODbL attribution and cannot be silently blended into CC BY public artifacts as raw database redistribution.

The recurring source registry marks both packages as non-count sources with dedicated metadata archive targets, so source freshness can monitor them without promoting them into outbreak_manifest case-count ingestion.
The local metadata file records resource IDs and handling instructions only; raw CSV/ZIP package contents remain out of the public repo by default.

chains.next_action: When a future snapshot is data-ready, derive health-zone population/mobility and map/access covariates from cached raw resources, run sensitivity analyses against the current hand-curated T3 covariates, and promote only if the effect is documented as a covariate sensitivity rather than a case-count update.

claim.locator: May 22 CDC/ECDC cross-check source entries and staged observations
claim.statement: CDC and ECDC May 22 public updates have been archived as official/regional cross-check evidence, while WHO May 22 remains the May 22 scored snapshot endpoint.
claim.value: CDC cross-check: 83 confirmed, 744 suspected, 176 suspected deaths. ECDC cross-check: 60 confirmed, 650 suspected, 160 deaths. CDC travel pages updated May 22 are context-only. No May 23 scored snapshot is triggered by these sources.

sources.citation: US CDC. Ebola Disease: Current Situation. 22 May 2026.
sources.finding: Official CDC cross-check reports 83 confirmed cases, 744 suspected cases, and 176 suspected deaths; this nearly corroborates but does not supersede the same-day WHO DG headline endpoint of 84/approximately 750/177.

sources.citation: ECDC. Ebola virus disease outbreak in DRC and Uganda. 22 May 2026 update.
sources.finding: Regional cross-check reports 60 confirmed cases, 650 suspected cases, and 160 deaths; lower than WHO/CDC and retained as lagged/regional cross-check evidence only.

sources.citation: US CDC. Interim Guidance: Public Health Assessment and Management of Travelers Arriving from Affected Countries During the 2026 Ebola Outbreak. 22 May 2026 page update.
sources.finding: Travel-monitoring guidance page updated May 22; archived as context-only, not a case-count or model input.

sources.citation: US CDC. What Travelers Need to Know About Returning to the United States from DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan. 22 May 2026 page update.
sources.finding: Returning-travelers public page updated May 22; archived as context-only, not a case-count or model input.

sources.citation: data/external_sources/freshness/bdbv-2026-2026-05-23.json generated by source_ingest.py --live-check.
sources.finding: Freshness report flagged May 22 CDC/ECDC/travel page updates and no newer primary WHO count endpoint after the May 22 WHO DG/IHR release.

steps.finding: CDC May 22 official tuple is one count below the WHO DG confirmed endpoint and near-identical on suspected and deaths, so it is corroborating cross-check evidence, not a headline replacement.
ECDC May 22 values are lower than WHO/CDC same-day values and are retained as regional review/cross-check evidence only.
CDC travel-management pages provide response/travel-monitoring context but no count tuple eligible for the model.
No source dated after 22 May is available from a primary official count source; the May 22 scored snapshot remains current after archiving these cross-check pages.

chains.next_action: Keep these May 22 sources in the manifest/export as provenance-complete cross-checks. Do not create a May 23 scored snapshot until a newer official primary count/geography source appears and passes the usual source-ingest/evidence-chain gates.
